background: The number of X-ray machine failures per week in a laboratory has a Poisson distribution with parameter λ=2. Currently facilities at this laboratory can repair two machines per week. Failures in excess of two will have to repair by an outside specialist contractor.

task.query: Determine the probability of having X-ray machines repaired by a specialist contractor on any given week?
task.answer:
The probability of having X-ray machines repaired by a specialist contractor is

$$
P(X \geq 3)=1-P(X \leq 2)
$$
$$
=1-\sum_{k=0}^2 \frac{2^k e^{-2}}{k!}=0.3232
$$
task.method: 事件发生概率及独立性

task.query: Determine the expected number of X-ray machines repaired by the specialist contractor per week?
task.answer:
The expected number of X-ray machines repaired by the specialist contractor is

$$
\sum_{k=3}^{\infty}(k-2) P(X=k)=\sum_{k=3}^{\infty}(k-2) \frac{2^k e^{-2}}{k!}=0.54
$$

meta_info.chapter: Chapter 2: Distribution Functions and Its Applications
